Maloo Results: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/related?jobs=lustre-reviews&builds=102395#redirect

Patch Set 1:

(3 comments)

I find this patch exceedingly difficult to review, as I have no way to see which Lustre changes might be removed.  Also, because the patches on master are reviewed by checkpatch.pl on the currently-landed version of the script, if this patch and the next one are not landed together, then there will be a time when there will be spurious warnings/errors generated. 

I was thinking it might be more useful to have an initial patch that generates a patch from the current checkpatch against the version from the kernel given in the header, and save it as contrib/scripts/checkpatch.pl.diff to preserve the current modifications, and then the second patch updates checkpatch.pl from the new kernel along with the checkpatch.pl.diff file as applied on top of the new script (ie. it wouldn't be the vanilla version of checkpatch.pl committed to the tree, but the modified version). 

That would both make it more clear what changes are being made to the Lustre version of the script, as well as preserve the .diff file as a record of those modifications and make them easier to apply in the future.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> (3 comments)
> 
> I find this patch exceedingly difficult to review, as I have no way to see which Lustre changes might be removed.  Also, because the patches on master are reviewed by checkpatch.pl on the currently-landed version of the script, if this patch and the next one are not landed together, then there will be a time when there will be spurious warnings/errors generated. 
> 
> I was thinking it might be more useful to have an initial patch that generates a patch from the current checkpatch against the version from the kernel given in the header, and save it as contrib/scripts/checkpatch.pl.diff to preserve the current modifications, and then the second patch updates checkpatch.pl from the new kernel along with the checkpatch.pl.diff file as applied on top of the new script (ie. it wouldn't be the vanilla version of checkpatch.pl committed to the tree, but the modified version). 
> 
> That would both make it more clear what changes are being made to the Lustre version of the script, as well as preserve the .diff file as a record of those modifications and make them easier to apply in the future.

Yeah, this is impossible to review - you can only validate that it's the same version upstream. Only the second patch is really reviewable. But this approach only works if Oleg lands both patches at the same time. I can try the diff approach.

Patch Set 4:

Tim, since this updated checkpatch.pl landed on 06-24, the check for "define OBD_CONNECT" is not working. For example, in patch v1 https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55404/1/lustre/include/uapi/linux/lustre/lustre_idl.h,unified the OBD_CONNECT warning is correctly generated, but in v2 and later of that patch (07-11) it is not generated. 
Similarly in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/55404 it is not displayed at all. 

I checked that the issue appears to be with the space in the left size of the expression in spelling.txt, and removing it generates the warning, but I don't want the warning for every use of OBD_CONNECT, only where it is defined. My perl-fu is not up to snuff to figure out why the regexp is not allowing the space in the match.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:

For example, in patch v1 https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55404/1/lustre/include/uapi/linux/lustre/lustre_idl.h,unified the OBD_CONNECT warning is correctly generated, but in v2 and later of that patch (07-11) it is not generated. 
> Similarly in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/55404 it is not displayed at all. 
> 
> I checked that the issue appears to be with the space in the left size of the expression in spelling.txt, and removing it generates the warning, but I don't want the warning for every use of OBD_CONNECT, only where it is defined. My perl-fu is not up to snuff to figure out why the regexp is not allowing the space in the match.

I'll take a look and see if I can fix it.

commit.subject: LU-6142 contrib: update checkpatch.pl to 6.10-rc2
commit.message:
LU-6142 contrib: update checkpatch.pl to 6.10-rc2

checkpatch.pl hasn't been updated in around 7 years. To make sure
we don't waste our efforts, update the script while porting a few
Lustre specific changes.

This updates checkpatch.pl to 6.10-rc2 while recording the changes
in contrib/.

This is mostly a simple port of the old changes to a new version of
checkpatch.pl. It is intended to silence a few false positives and
generate a few Lustre specific warnings.

One feature has been added: the ability to differentiate between
user space code and kernel code when doing spell checks.

I tested this by running the updated script across the entire tree,
ensuring that nothing failed:

contrib:         loc=192    errors/warnings=1
libcfs:          loc=25117  errors/warnings=1624
lnet:            loc=133290 errors/warnings=16381
lustre:          loc=645087 errors/warnings=81360

Also, add a userspace_spelling.txt file by copying spelling.txt. We
start by simply duplicating the old spelling.txt file while removing
a number of entries.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Change-Id: Ia39c7f04d2407786f158d7f6f95968e182e99ebf
